examples.title: Basic main() with Command Line Arguments
examples.description: Demonstrates standard main() function signature with argc and argv to process command-line arguments
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates the standard main() signature. argc holds the count of arguments, and argv is an array of pointers to the argument strings. argv[0] is always the program name. The loop starts from i=1 to skip the program name.

examples.title: Accessing Environment Variables via main()
examples.description: Shows how to access environment variables using the third parameter of main()
examples.explanation: The third parameter envp[] provides access to environment variables. Each element points to a string in 'NAME=value' format. The array is NULL-terminated, so we iterate until we find NULL. This is an alternative to using the global 'environ' variable.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of env vars

examples.title: Program with Exit Status and Error Handling
examples.description: Demonstrates proper exit status handling based on command-line argument validation
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates proper exit status handling: return 1 indicates an error (missing required argument), return 0 indicates success. The program also shows how to parse command-line flags and display usage information. The exit status can be checked in shell using $?.
